context: Garuda asks Lord Vishnu about the fate of the soul after death. Vishnu describes the terrifying journey of the sinful soul along the 86,000-yojana path to Yama's realm, the appearance of the Yamadoots, the soul's departure from the body, and the supreme importance of righteous living.

verses.hindi:
श्रीगरुड उवाच —
भगवन् सर्वधर्मज्ञ सर्वशास्त्रविशारद ।
मृत्युकाले तु जीवानां या गतिस्तां वदस्व मे ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Garuḍa uvāca —
Bhagavan sarvadharmajña sarvaśāstraviśārada |
mṛtyukāle tu jīvānāṃ yā gatistāṃ vadasva me ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Lord, knower of all dharmas, master of all scriptures, tell me what becomes of the soul at the time of death.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि यमलोकस्य निर्णयम् ।
प्रेतत्वं च यथा जीवो मृत्युकाले तु विन्दति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi yamalokasya nirṇayam |
pretatvaṃ ca yathā jīvo mṛtyukāle tu vindati ||
verses.translation: The Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya (Garuda), I shall describe the nature of Yama's realm, and how the soul attains the state of a departed spirit at the time of death.

verses.hindi:
धर्मेण जायते जन्तुरधर्मेण विनश्यति ।
धर्मार्थं सकलं चेष्टं धर्मः सारो हि देहिनाम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Dharmeṇa jāyate jantur adharmeṇa vinaśyati |
dharmārthaṃ sakalaṃ ceṣṭaṃ dharmaḥ sāro hi dehinām ||
verses.translation: By dharma a being is born, and by adharma it perishes. All effort should be directed toward dharma, for dharma is the essence of embodied life.

verses.hindi:
मृत्युकाले यदा जीवो देहत्यागं करोति हि ।
सम्प्राप्ते काले निर्याति प्राणो दुःखेन भो खग ॥
verses.transliteration:
Mṛtyukāle yadā jīvo dehatyāgaṃ karoti hi |
samprāpte kāle niryāti prāṇo duḥkhena bho khaga ||
verses.translation: When the appointed time of death arrives and the soul abandons the body, the life-breath departs with great suffering, O Bird.

verses.hindi:
अधर्मिणां च जीवानां मरणं भयदं भवेत् ।
भूतग्रामो निवर्तेत कम्पते विकलेन्द्रियः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Adharmiṇāṃ ca jīvānāṃ maraṇaṃ bhayadaṃ bhavet |
bhūtagrāmo nivarteta kampate vikalendriyaḥ ||
verses.translation: For the unrighteous, death is terrifying. The aggregate of the elements begins to dissolve, the senses fail, and the body trembles.

verses.hindi:
ततो यमस्य दूतौ द्वौ भीमरूपौ भयानकौ ।
पापिनं प्राप्य तिष्ठेते पाशहस्तौ भयंकरौ ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tato yamasya dūtau dvau bhīmarūpau bhayānakau |
pāpinaṃ prāpya tiṣṭhete pāśahastau bhayaṃkarau ||
verses.translation: Then two messengers of Yama, of terrible and fearsome form, arrive before the sinner, holding nooses in their hands, terrifying to behold.

verses.hindi:
कृष्णवर्णौ विरूपाक्षौ नखदंष्ट्रायुधौ भृशम् ।
कालपाशधरौ घोरौ कठोरौ दुर्निरीक्षणौ ॥
verses.transliteration:
Kṛṣṇavarṇau virūpākṣau nakhadaṃṣṭrāyudhau bhṛśam |
kālapāśadharau ghorau kaṭhorau durnirīkṣaṇau ||
verses.translation: They are dark-complexioned, with deformed eyes, armed with nails and fangs, bearing the noose of death, fierce, harsh, and dreadful to look upon.

verses.hindi:
ततः पापी भयत्रस्तो मलमूत्रं विमुञ्चति ।
अङ्गुष्ठमात्रो भवति जीवः काले विनिर्गतः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tataḥ pāpī bhayatrasto malamūtraṃ vimuñcati |
aṅguṣṭhamātro bhavati jīvaḥ kāle vinirgatah ||
verses.translation: Then the sinner, stricken with fear, loses control of bodily functions. The soul, no bigger than a thumb, emerges from the body at the appointed time.

verses.hindi:
यमदूतैस्तु दृष्ट्वा तं पापिनं विकलेन्द्रियम् ।
पाशैर्बद्ध्वा बलात् तेन नीयते यमसादनम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yamadūtaistu dṛṣṭvā taṃ pāpinaṃ vikalendriyam |
pāśair baddhvā balāt tena nīyate yamasādanam ||
verses.translation: The messengers of Yama, seeing that sinful soul bereft of senses, bind it with nooses and forcibly drag it to the abode of Yama.

verses.hindi:
षडशीतिसहस्राणि योजनानां ततो गतिः ।
यमस्य नगरं यावत् तत् पन्थानं व्रजेत् प्रभो ॥
verses.transliteration:
Ṣaḍaśītisahasrāṇi yojanānāṃ tato gatiḥ |
yamasya nagaraṃ yāvat tat panthānaṃ vrajet prabho ||
verses.translation: The path to the city of Yama extends eighty-six thousand yojanas, and the soul must traverse this entire dreadful road.

verses.hindi:
अत्युष्णवालुकामार्गे तीक्ष्णकण्टकसंकुले ।
दावाग्निदग्धवसुधे अन्धकारे भयानके ॥
verses.transliteration:
Atyuṣṇavālukāmārge tīkṣṇakaṇṭakasaṃkule |
dāvāgnidagdhavasudhe andhakāre bhayānake ||
verses.translation: The path is covered with scorching sand, strewn with sharp thorns, the ground is burned by forest fires, and it is engulfed in fearsome darkness.

verses.hindi:
न छाया तत्र वृक्षाणां न जलं न विश्रामभूः ।
क्षुत्तृड्भ्यां पीडितो जीवो यमदूतैः प्रताड्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Na chāyā tatra vṛkṣāṇāṃ na jalaṃ na viśrāmabhūḥ |
kṣuttṛḍbhyāṃ pīḍito jīvo yamadūtaiḥ pratāḍyate ||
verses.translation: There is no shade of trees, no water, no resting place. Tormented by hunger and thirst, the soul is beaten by the messengers of Yama.

verses.hindi:
तत्र वैतरणी नाम नदी पापभयंकरी ।
पूयशोणितपूर्णा च मांसमज्जासमाकुला ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tatra Vaitaraṇī nāma nadī pāpabhayaṃkarī |
pūyaśoṇitapūrṇā ca māṃsamajjāsamākulā ||
verses.translation: There flows the river Vaitarani, terrifying to sinners, filled with pus and blood, with flesh and marrow.

verses.hindi:
गोदानेन तरत्येवं वैतरणीं सुदुर्गमाम् ।
तस्मात् गोदानमेव स्याद् धर्मः श्रेष्ठो मतो मम ॥
verses.transliteration:
Godānena taraty evaṃ Vaitaraṇīṃ sudurgamām |
tasmāt godānam eva syād dharmaḥ śreṣṭho mato mama ||
verses.translation: By the gift of a cow, one crosses the impassable Vaitarani. Therefore the gift of a cow is considered the highest dharma, in my view.

verses.hindi:
दानं तपो व्रतं शीलं यज्ञः सत्यं दया क्षमा ।
मृत्युकाले सहायन्ते धर्म एकोऽनुगच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Dānaṃ tapo vrataṃ śīlaṃ yajñaḥ satyaṃ dayā kṣamā |
mṛtyukāle sahāyante dharma eko'nugacchati ||
verses.translation: Charity, austerity, vows, good conduct, sacrifice, truth, compassion, and forgiveness — at the time of death these support one. Dharma alone follows after.

verses.hindi:
बन्धुजनाः श्मशाने तु देहं त्यक्त्वा निवर्तयन् ।
धर्मस्तमनुगच्छेत् तु यत्र कुत्रापि गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Bandhujanaḥ śmaśāne tu dehaṃ tyaktvā nivartayan |
dharmastam anugacchet tu yatra kutrāpi gacchati ||
verses.translation: Kinsmen turn back from the cremation ground after abandoning the body, but dharma follows the soul wherever it goes.

verses.hindi:
यथा गौर्वत्सकं स्वं तु बहूनां मध्यतो वनम् ।
तथा पूर्वकृतं कर्म कर्तारमनुगच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yathā gaur vatsakaṃ svaṃ tu bahūnāṃ madhyato vanam |
tathā pūrvakṛtaṃ karma kartāram anugacchati ||
verses.translation: Just as a cow finds her own calf among many in the forest, so does one's past karma follow its doer.

verses.hindi:
धर्मिणां च यदा मृत्युः तदा दिव्यविमानकम् ।
आयाति सहसा तत्र पुष्पवृष्टिसमन्वितम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Dharmiṇāṃ ca yadā mṛtyuḥ tadā divyavimānakam |
āyāti sahasā tatra puṣpavṛṣṭisamanvitam ||
verses.translation: When the righteous die, a celestial chariot arrives swiftly, accompanied by a shower of flowers.

verses.hindi:
विष्णुदूताश्च भद्राक्षाः पीतवस्त्रधराः शुभाः ।
धर्मिणं प्राप्य तिष्ठन्ति विष्णुलोकं नयन्ति ते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Viṣṇudūtāśca bhadrākṣāḥ pītavastradharāḥ śubhāḥ |
dharmiṇaṃ prāpya tiṣṭhanti Viṣṇulokaṃ nayanti te ||
verses.translation: The messengers of Vishnu, with auspicious eyes and wearing yellow garments, come before the righteous one and lead the soul to the realm of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
पापिनस्तु भयं प्राप्य रुदन्ति विलपन्ति च ।
न च कश्चित् सहायस्तु तत्र तेषां भवेद् ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Pāpinastu bhayaṃ prāpya rudanti vilapanti ca |
na ca kaścit sahāyastu tatra teṣāṃ bhaved dhruvam ||
verses.translation: But the sinful, seized by terror, weep and wail, and indeed not a single helper is found for them there.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन धर्ममेव समाचरेत् ।
शरीरं क्षणभङ्गुर्हि धर्मः सारो हि देहिनाम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmāt sarvaprayatnena dharmam eva samācaret |
śarīraṃ kṣaṇabhaṅgurhi dharmaḥ sāro hi dehinām ||
verses.translation: Therefore, with every effort one should practice dharma. The body is perishable in an instant; dharma alone is the essence for embodied beings.

verses.hindi:
पिण्डदानं तथा दानं श्राद्धं चापि विधानतः ।
कुर्वन्ति ये नरास्तस्य सुगतिं यान्ति ते सदा ॥
verses.transliteration:
Piṇḍadānaṃ tathā dānaṃ śrāddhaṃ cāpi vidhānataḥ |
kurvanti ye narāstasya sugatiṃ yānti te sadā ||
verses.translation: Those who offer the pinda (rice balls), give charity, and perform the shraddha rites according to the prescribed method — they always attain a good destination.

verses.hindi:
दशाहं च तथा कर्म प्रेतस्य हितकाम्यया ।
पिण्डदानादिकं कुर्यात् तेन प्रेतो विमुच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Daśāhaṃ ca tathā karma pretasya hitakāmyayā |
piṇḍadānādikaṃ kuryāt tena preto vimucyate ||
verses.translation: For ten days, with the desire to benefit the departed, one should offer pinda and other rites. By this the departed soul is liberated from its ghostly state.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
इति ते कथितं तार्क्ष्य मार्गं यमपुरस्य च ।
धर्मस्य महिमा ज्ञेयो मया प्रोक्तो हि तेऽखिलः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Iti te kathitaṃ Tārkṣya mārgaṃ yamapurasya ca |
dharmasya mahimā jñeyo mayā prokto hi te'khilaḥ ||
verses.translation: The Lord said: Thus I have described to you, O Tarksya, the path to Yama's city, and the glory of dharma I have told you in full.
